baker-cyclicality-thesis.rich:
Gavin Baker (@GavinSBaker, Atreides Management) articulated a framework for semiconductor investing in a November 24, 2023 tweet thread and expanded on it in his December 2025 "Invest Like the Best" podcast (EP.451). The core claims:

1. Semiconductor companies **trade like the cyclicals they have always been** — even the most secular, highest-growth names experience massive inventory corrections every 2-3 years. 2. **Any semiconductor company whose products are in short supply will see significant multiple compression.** Elevated lead times = lower multiples. 3. The converse may also be true: stocks may **hold up better or even rise** when revenue declines 20-40% from peak. 4. **Every shortage is followed by a glut.** (Paraphrasing Nassim Taleb: "I've seen gluts not followed by shortages, but I've never seen a shortage not followed by a glut.")

Baker calls this the **"iron law of economic history"**: semiconductor inventory cycles are inevitable because customer buffer inventories must equal lead times. When lead times extend, customers double-order. When supply catches up, cancellations cascade.

This output evaluates Baker's claims against 6 semiconductor subsectors from 2019-2026, using specific tickers, price data, P/E multiples, revenue figures, and lead time data.

---

##The Evidence Matrix

| Subsector | Tickers | Shortage Period | Glut Period | Revenue Peak-to-Trough | Stock Peak-to-Trough | P/E at Peak Earnings | P/E at Trough Earnings | Baker Thesis Confirmed? | | -------------------- | ------- | ------------------ | ------------------- | --------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ---------------------- | ----------------------- | | **Memory (Cycle 2)** | MU | 2021-2022 | 2022-2023 | -50% ($30.8B -> $15.5B) | -50% ($98 -> $49) | 4x | 145x (negative EPS) | Yes — textbook | | **Memory (Cycle 3)** | MU | 2025-2026 | TBD | Record $23.9B/qtr | -18% ($461 -> $377 so far) | 10-12x fwd (compressing now) | TBD | Repeating | | **Foundry** | TSM | 2020-2021 | 2022-2023 | -22% quarterly ($20.2B -> $15.7B) | -58% ($142 -> $60) | 20-22x (down from 30x) | 16-18x | Yes | | **Equipment** | ASML | 2021-2022 | 2023-2024 | Revenue grew (backlog lag) | -55% ($850 -> $363) | 88x (bookings peak) | 33-35x | Yes — via bookings | | **Equipment** | LRCX | 2021-2022 | 2023-2024 | -14.5% ($17.4B -> $14.9B) | -57% ($700 -> $340) | 20-25x | 37x | Yes — inverted P/E | | **Auto/Analog** | TXN | 2021-2022 | 2023-2024 | -22% ($20.0B -> $15.6B) | Stock HIGHER in glut | **16x (decade low)** | **38x** | Yes — strongest proof | | **Auto/Analog** | ON | 2021-2022 | 2023-2025 | -28% ($8.3B -> $6.0B) | -48% ($108 -> $56) | 14-17x | 187x | Yes | | **Auto/Analog** | ADI | 2021-2023 | 2023-2024 | -23% ($12.3B -> $9.4B) | Moderate | 25x | 68x (decade high) | Yes | | **GPU** | NVDA | 2020-2021 (gaming) | 2022 (crypto crash) | -54% quarterly gaming | -66% ($33 -> $11 adj.) | ~60-80x (compressed) | ~175x trailing | Yes | | **GPU** | NVDA | 2023-present (AI) | TBD | N/A (still rising) | N/A | 21x fwd (compressing) | TBD | Compressing now | | **Broadcom** | AVGO | Mild 2021 | Mild 2022 | Revenue never declined | -36% ($66 -> $42 adj.) | ~38x | ~19x | **Partial exception** |

---

##Subsector Deep Dives

##1. Memory — MU (The Canonical Cycle)

The DRAM/NAND cycle is Baker's Exhibit A. Three complete cycles in 8 years:

**Cycle 1 (2017-2020):** Shortage: DRAM prices tripled 2016-2018. MU revenue hit $30.4B (FY2018), EPS $11.51. Glut: Revenue fell 30% to $21.4B by FY2020. DRAM prices fell 50-60%. **Trailing P/E at peak earnings: 2.96x** — the lowest in the S&P 500. The market screamed "these earnings aren't sustainable." Stock fell 56% ($64 -> $28).

**Cycle 2 (2020-2023):** Shortage: WFH + 5G + gaming demand. Revenue back to $30.8B (FY2022). Lead times stretched to 20-30 weeks. Glut: Revenue **halved** to $15.5B (FY2023). Net income swung from +$8.7B to -$5.8B. Inventory hit 31 weeks of supply. Stock fell 50% ($98 -> $49). P/E went from ~4x (peak) to 145x/negative (trough).

**Cycle 3 (2024-present):** Shortage: AI/HBM demand. Revenue hit $23.9B in a single quarter (Q2 FY2026). EPS $12.20. All vendors sold out through 2026. **Forward P/E already compressing to 10-12x** despite 39% earnings beat. Stock pulled back from $461 to $377 despite record-shattering results. Baker position: Long MU $411M common + $200M put hedge. He believes his own thesis about the inevitable cycle turn. Structural differences this cycle: HBM uses 3x wafer area per bit, only 3 suppliers globally, non-cancellable contracts. But all three vendors expanding capacity simultaneously — the classic setup for the next glut.

**The Paradox (all three cycles):** Buy MU when P/E is high/negative (trough), sell when P/E is low (peak). This is the opposite of how most investors think about valuation.

##2. Foundry — TSM

TSMC's 2019-2026 trajectory is textbook Baker:

**2021 shortage:** Utilization 100%+ across all nodes. Lead times 20-30 weeks. Forward P/E compressed from ~30x (early 2021) to ~20x (late 2021) despite 18% revenue growth. Stock went sideways from Feb 2021 peak of $142 through year-end. **2022-2023 glut:** Utilization dropped to ~65-80%. Mature node utilization fell below 60%. Capex cut 15% (TSMC almost never cuts capex). Revenue fell ~22% peak-to-trough quarterly. **Stock bottomed at $60 in Oct 2022** — 9 months before the revenue trough (Q2 2023). Revenue was still HIGHER at the stock trough than at the stock peak. **2023 recovery:** Stock gained 40% while revenue was still declining. The market "looked through" the trough to AI-driven recovery. **2024-2026:** Stock rallied to $200-212. 3.5x from trough. N3/N5 utilization back to 100%+.

**Key data point:** Stock peaked (Feb 2021 at $142) **18 months before** revenue peaked (Q3 2022 at $20.2B). Stock troughed (Oct 2022 at $60) **9 months before** revenue troughed (Q2 2023 at $15.7B). The stock leads fundamentals in both directions.

Cycle length from peak shortage (mid-2021) to peak glut (mid-2023): **exactly 2 years** — matching Baker's framework.

##3. Equipment — ASML, LRCX, AMAT

Equipment companies sit at the top of the capex cascade — they see boom orders first and cancellations first.

**ASML (the purest example):** Bookings surged to EUR 8.9B/quarter (Q3 2022) during peak shortage. P/E hit **88x** — highest in a decade. Bookings collapsed to EUR 2.6B (Q3 2024) — a 71% decline. Stock fell 55% even though *revenue kept growing* (backlog lag of 2+ years). Oct 15, 2024: Bookings missed consensus by 53%. Stock fell 15.7% in one session — worst day since IPO. The market punished ASML not for bad current earnings, but for the implication that the order boom was fading.

**LRCX (memory-exposed):** 47% of revenue from NAND at peak. When memory customers cut capex ~40%, NAND spending collapsed >75%. Stock fell 56.8% — steepest of the three equipment names. **Inverted P/E pattern:** P/E was lowest (~20-25x) during the revenue boom and highest (~37x) at the revenue trough. Classic cyclical stock behavior — the market pays a premium on trough earnings.

**AMAT (dampened cycle):** Revenue never actually declined — growth decelerated from +34% to +3%. China front-loading before export controls masked organic downturn. Stock still fell 49% despite steady earnings. P/E compressed to 18.6x (below 10-year average).

**Meta-pattern across all three:** Stock peaked 6-12 months before revenue peaked, and troughed 6-12 months before revenue troughed. Watch bookings, not revenue.

##4. Auto/Analog — TXN, ON, NXPI, ADI (The Clearest Proof)

The 2021 auto chip shortage is the most visible shortage/glut cycle in recent history and the **strongest evidence** for Baker's thesis.

**The shortage (2020-2022):** Auto OEMs canceled chip orders during COVID (Q2 2020), then scrambled to re-order (Q4 2020). Foundries had already reallocated capacity. Compounding shocks: Texas deep freeze, Renesas fire, Malaysian COVID outbreaks. Lead times stretched from ~12 weeks (normal) to **27+ weeks** (May 2022 peak). Some auto MCUs hit 38-52+ weeks. Double/triple ordering amplified the bullwhip effect. Revenue surged: TXN +27% (2021), ON +28%, NXPI +28%.

**The glut (2023-2024):** Customers consumed 6-12 months of safety stock, stopped ordering. Mobileye discovered customers had accumulated 6-7 million excess EyeQ chips. Lead times collapsed from 27+ weeks to <15 weeks by Q4 2023. Some analog parts went below 8 weeks — shorter than pre-pandemic norms. Revenue declines: TXN -22% ($20.0B -> $15.6B), ON -28% ($8.3B -> $6.0B), ADI -23%.

**The P/E data — Baker's thesis in a single data point:**

| TXN | Peak Earnings (Q3 2022) | Trough Earnings (Q3 2024) | |-----|------------------------|--------------------------| | Revenue | $20.0B/yr | $15.6B/yr | | EPS | $9.65 | $5.42 | | Stock Price | $154.78 | $206.57 | | **P/E** | **16.0x (decade low)** | **38.1x** |

TXN's stock was **$52 higher** during the glut ($207) than at peak earnings ($155). The P/E more than doubled from 16x to 38x. The market assigned the lowest multiple when earnings were highest, and the highest multiple when earnings were lowest. This is the Peter Lynch / Gavin Baker pattern: **buy cyclicals at high P/E (depressed earnings), sell at low P/E (peak earnings).**

ON Semiconductor was even more extreme: P/E went from 14-17x at peak earnings to **187x** at the trough (EPS collapsed to $0.29/quarter).

##5. GPU — NVDA, AMD

The GPU market experienced the fastest shortage-to-glut-to-shortage transition in semiconductor history.

**Gaming/Crypto shortage (2020-2021):** RTX 3080 launched at $699, sold for $1,400-$2,000+ (200%+ MSRP). 25% of GPU shipments went to crypto miners. NVDA gaming revenue peaked at $12.46B (FY2022). Stock ran from ~$6 to ~$33 (split-adjusted), a 5.5x move.

**Crypto crash glut (2022):** Ethereum Merge (Sep 15, 2022): GPU mining became obsolete overnight. Millions of mining GPUs flooded the market. NVDA gaming revenue crashed 54% quarterly ($3.42B -> $1.57B). Took ~$2B+ in inventory write-downs. Stock fell 66% to $11.22 (split-adjusted) in Oct 2022. Trailing P/E hit ~175x (collapsed earnings). **Glut duration: ~12 months** from onset to inventory normalization.

**AI shortage (2023-present):** ChatGPT launched Nov 30, 2022 — while NVIDIA was still clearing inventory. H100 lead times exceeded 50+ weeks by mid-2023. Data center revenue: $3.6B/quarter (Jan 2023) -> $62.3B/quarter (Jan 2026). **17x increase in 3 years.** Stock went from $11 to $148+ (13x). But earnings grew ~10x, so the **forward P/E actually compressed from 60x+ to 21x**. Baker's thesis confirmed: the market refuses to capitalize shortage-level earnings at peak multiples.

**Baker's position:** $1.01B in NVDA ($653M calls + $359M common) — his highest-conviction bet. $2.2B QQQ puts as cycle hedge. Exited AMD entirely (winner-take-most conviction). He acknowledges the "iron law" applies to AI compute but believes demand growth will outpace supply additions for longer than expected.

##6. Broadcom — AVGO (The Counter-Example)

AVGO largely **avoided** the classic shortage/glut cycle through two mechanisms: 1. **Hock Tan's supply discipline:** Deliberately under-shipped during the 2021 shortage, preventing downstream inventory buildup. When peers saw orders collapse in 2022-2023, Broadcom's drawdown was minimal. 2. **Software revenue ballast:** 42% of revenue from subscription software (VMware, CA, Symantec) doesn't cycle with semiconductor demand.

Revenue never actually declined in any year. The 2022 correction (-36% stock decline) was driven by Fed rate hikes and VMware deal uncertainty, not a business downturn. P/E compressed to 19.4x (5-year low) then re-expanded to 67x as the market repriced AVGO as a secular AI + software compounder.

**Baker does not hold AVGO.** It's already priced as a secular compounder, not a cyclical mispricing. Baker prefers to deploy capital into pure-play bottleneck names at compressed multiples (COHR, ALAB, MU).

**Lesson:** Companies can partially escape the cycle through supply discipline and revenue diversification — but even AVGO's stock gets hit by sector-wide sentiment during downturns.

---

##The Iron Laws — Synthesized

From Baker's public commentary (Nov 2023 tweet thread, Dec 2025 "Invest Like the Best" EP.451) and the evidence above:

##Law 1: Every shortage is followed by a glut

**Confirmed across all 6 subsectors.** The cycle length from peak shortage to peak glut ranges from 12-24 months, consistent with Baker's 2-3 year framework (peak-to-trough-to-new-peak is 3-5 years).

| Subsector | Shortage Peak | Glut Trough | Duration | |-----------|--------------|-------------|----------| | Memory (Cycle 1) | H1 2018 | H1 2019 | ~12 months | | Memory (Cycle 2) | H1 2022 | H1 2023 | ~12 months | | Foundry (TSM) | Mid-2021 | Mid-2023 | ~24 months | | Equipment (ASML) | Q3 2022 (bookings) | Q3 2024 (bookings) | ~24 months | | Auto/Analog | May 2022 (lead times) | Q4 2023 (lead times) | ~18 months | | GPU (gaming/crypto) | Late 2021 | Mid-2022 | ~6-9 months |

##Law 2: Multiple compression during shortage

**Confirmed across all subsectors** — the most counterintuitive finding:

| Ticker | P/E at Peak Earnings | P/E at Trough Earnings | Multiple Expansion at Trough | |--------|---------------------|----------------------|----------------------------| | MU | 2.96x (FY2018) | 145x / negative (FY2023) | 49x+ | | TSM | 20-22x (2021) | 12-13x (Oct 2022) then 16-18x (2023) | Compressed at both extremes | | ASML | 88x (Q1 2022, bookings peak) | 33-35x (2023-2024) | Actually compressed at trough too (rate hikes) | | TXN | **16x (Q3 2022)** | **38x (Q3 2024)** | **2.4x expansion** | | ON | 14-17x (2022-2023) | 187x (late 2025) | 11-13x expansion | | ADI | 25x (FY2023) | 68x (FY2024) | 2.7x expansion | | NVDA | 21x fwd (2025-2026) | 175x trailing (Oct 2022) | Dramatic |

**The meta-lesson:** For cyclical semiconductors, a low P/E is a sell signal and a high P/E is a buy signal. The market correctly anticipates the cycle turn and refuses to capitalize peak earnings at growth multiples.

##Law 3: Stocks lead fundamentals by 6-12 months

Consistent across every subsector: **TSM:** Stock peaked Feb 2021, revenue peaked Q3 2022 (18-month lead). Stock troughed Oct 2022, revenue troughed Q2 2023 (9-month lead). . **TXN:** Stock peaked Nov 2021, revenue peaked Q3 2022 (10-month lead). Stock essentially troughed in late 2023, revenue troughed Q1 2024 (3-6 month lead). **ASML:** Stock peaked Nov 2021, bookings peaked Q3 2022 (10-month lead).

##Law 4: Lead times are the signal

Baker's actionable indicator: **rising lead times = sell the rally; falling lead times = buy the dip.**

| Subsector | Normal Lead Time | Shortage Peak | Glut Trough | |-----------|-----------------|---------------|-------------| | Auto/Analog | 8-12 weeks | 27-52+ weeks | <8 weeks (below normal) | | Memory (DRAM) | 8-12 weeks | 20-30 weeks | Inventory 31 weeks of supply | | Foundry (TSMC) | 10-14 weeks | 20-30 weeks | 10-12 weeks | | GPU (H100) | N/A | 50+ weeks | 8-12 weeks |

---

##Counter-Arguments — Why "This Time Is Different" (Maybe)

The strongest counter-arguments to Baker's framework being applied to the current AI cycle:

1. **Concentrated supply:** Leading-edge logic (TSMC ~90%), HBM (3 vendors), EUV (ASML monopoly). Fewer players = more supply discipline = less violent gluts than when 15+ DRAM makers competed in the 1990s.

2. **Capital intensity:** A leading-edge fab costs $20-40B and takes 3-5 years. Supply additions are slower and more deliberate than in prior cycles, potentially extending shortage phases.

3. **Demand diversity:** AI compute demand comes from training (lumpy) AND inference (steady-state, growing). Even if training plateaus, inference may sustain utilization. Baker himself acknowledges: "every time you use AI takes compute in a way that traditional software just did not."

4. **Non-cancellable contracts:** HBM and CoWoS capacity sold under long-term agreements. This reduces but doesn't eliminate the inventory cycle.

5. **Geopolitical constraints:** Export controls, CHIPS Act subsidies, and reshoring create artificial supply constraints that wouldn't exist in a purely market-driven cycle.

**Baker's response:** He doesn't dispute these structural differences. He hedges with puts ($2.2B QQQ) rather than exiting the trade. His framework says the *direction* of the cycle is predictable even if the *timing* is not. The current cycle may be longer and larger, but the pattern will repeat.

Notable skeptics and allies: **Stanley Druckenmiller:** Reduced NVDA in 2024, citing valuation and cycle concerns **Dylan Patel (SemiAnalysis):** Tends bullish on duration of AI buildout but acknowledges inventory cycle risk **WSTS data:** Global semi industry has shown consistent 3-5 year peak-to-peak cycles for 40+ years (downturns: 1985, 1990-91, 1996-97, 2001, 2008-09, 2012, 2015-16, 2019, 2022-23)

---

##Baker's Playbook — How He Trades It

Synthesized from his public commentary, 13F filings, and podcast appearances:

1. **Buy when:** Industry is in a downturn, inventories elevated, stocks de-rated, sentiment terrible, capex being cut. *High P/E on depressed earnings.* 2. **Sell/hedge when:** Lead times extend, capex guidance rises, euphoric sentiment, "this time is different" narratives. *Low P/E on peak earnings.* 3. **Use options, not binary bets:** Long common + put hedge (e.g., MU $411M long + $200M puts). Calls for leverage when conviction is high (NVDA $653M in calls). The cycle is predictable in direction but not timing — puts protect against being early. 4. **Concentrate on the dominant player during shortage:** In a supply-constrained market, the #1 player captures disproportionate value. Hence NVDA over AMD (exited), and COHR over smaller optical names. 5. **Macro hedge the portfolio:** $2.2B QQQ puts protect the entire AI infra portfolio against a broad market downturn or cycle turn, without requiring precise timing.

---

##Implications for the {{concepts/bottleneck-cascade}}

Baker's cyclicality framework intersects with the bottleneck cascade thesis:

| Cascade Stage | Current Status | Baker Cycle Risk | Lead Time Signal | |---------------|---------------|-----------------|-----------------| | CoWoS packaging | Played out | Cycle complete — shortage resolved | Normal | | Power / DC buildout | Played out | Overcapacity emerging (56 GW announced) | Normal | | {{concepts/memory-supercycle}} | **Active** | **P/E compressing (10-12x fwd). All vendors expanding. Classic peak setup.** | **Tight (8 weeks inventory, 70% fill rates)** | | N3 logic wafers | Active | Utilization 100%+. Intel empty fabs = future supply. | Extending | | {{concepts/pluggable-optics}} | Active | Revenue flowing now. Less cyclical than memory (newer market). | Unknown | | {{concepts/co-packaged-optics}} | Next | Volume is years away — no cycle risk yet | N/A | | EUV tools | Next | ASML bookings just hit record EUR 13.2B (Q4 2025) — watch for reversal | Extending |

**The current risk:** Memory and N3 are both showing shortage-phase characteristics (tight supply, extending lead times, expanding capex). Baker's framework predicts multiple compression is already underway (MU forward P/E compressing from 20x+ to 10-12x) and a glut phase in 2027-2028 if supply additions overshoot demand.

---

nvda-swing-trade-thesis.rich:
Query: Can we exploit the $170-$190 range in NVDA before earnings, via a swing trade and/or an iron condor?

**Verdict: Yes — the range is technically confirmed and fundamentally supported, but the window closes at earnings (~May 28).**

##Thesis Summary

NVDA is trading at ~$177.64, pinned between incredible fundamentals (21x forward P/E, 73% YoY revenue growth, $97B FCF) and macro headwinds (25% semiconductor tariffs, FTC antitrust probe, China competition). This tug-of-war creates a defined trading range.

**$170 is confirmed support** — tested 3x in Q1 2026, bounced each time **$185-190 is confirmed resistance** — 50-day MA ($182.8) and 200-day MA ($184) form a ceiling; death cross in place **Baker holds $1B+** including $902M in new call options — a whale buyer defending dips **Leopold exited entirely** — rotated into CRWV, LITE, power names

##Earnings History Pattern

NVDA has beaten on both revenue and EPS for 8 consecutive quarters. But 4 of 8 post-earnings reactions were negative despite beating — short-term traders sell the event, institutions accumulate on dips.

##Iron Condor: Range-Bound Options Play

The thesis "NVDA stays between $170 and $190" maps to an **iron condor** — selling volatility within a defined range.

##Structure

| Leg | Strike | Action | |-----|--------|--------| | Long put (protection) | $160 | Buy | | Short put (floor) | $170 | Sell | | Short call (ceiling) | $190 | Sell | | Long call (protection) | $200 | Buy | | **Expiration** | **May 16, 2026** | **Before earnings** |

##Risk/Reward

| Metric | Value | |--------|-------| | Estimated net credit | ~$2.40 – $3.00 ($240-300/contract) | | Max loss | ~$7.00 – $7.60 ($700-760/contract) | | Lower break-even | ~$167.30 | | Upper break-even | ~$192.70 | | Probability of profit | ~55-65% | | Capital at risk | ~$730/contract |

##Management Rules

Close at 50% of max profit (~$135) to improve win rate Close if loss reaches 2x credit (~$540) — don't ride to max loss Need ~3 wins to offset 1 max loss at full width

##Why May 16 Expiration

39 DTE = sweet spot for theta decay **Expires before earnings (~May 28)** — avoids the 8-12% binary move Monthly expiration = best liquidity, tightest bid-ask spreads IV rank is low (12.10) so premium isn't huge, but the range is tight enough to work

##What Breaks the Range

**Upward (above $190):** Q1 FY2027 earnings beat + guidance raise (late May) Rubin production ahead of schedule Major new AI customer announcement Tariff reduction / trade deal

**Downward (below $170):** New tariff escalation (50%+ or full China ban) FTC antitrust enforcement action (not just investigation) Hyperscaler capex cut announcement Broader market crash (SPY -10%+)

##Thesis-Level Grounding

From {{concepts/bottleneck-cascade}}: NVDA sits at the intersection of three active bottlenecks — {{concepts/n3-wafer-crunch}}, {{concepts/memory-supercycle}}, and {{concepts/pluggable-optics}}. The {{concepts/token-economics}} framework ("compute = revenue") means GPU demand compounds indefinitely. At 21x forward P/E with $500B revenue visibility and $95.2B in supply commitments, the fundamental floor is real. The stock is compressed by temporary headwinds (tariffs, sentiment), not structural deterioration.

{{sources/semianalysis-signals}}: N3 utilization 100%+ in H2 2026, AI = 60% of output (86% by 2027). NVDA has the strongest demand curve against constrained supply.

The swing and the iron condor are complementary. The swing captures directional upside ($170 → $185 = ~8%). The iron condor captures time decay within the range. Both close before earnings.
